answers:
**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed before it goes out?**

No — as currently written, four of the document's claims should not go out:

- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows."** (`highlights-v4.md:5`) — I found zero supporting evidence anywhere in this repository or the wider 15-repo workspace (no metrics data, no changelog, no analytics export mentions this figure). It appears nowhere except the doc itself. This is an unsubstantiated statistic and must not be sent without a citable source.
- **"The scheduling calendar has been redesigned end to end (shown with production data)"** (`highlights-v4.md:16-17`) — false. `docs/release/captures.json` records both scheduling screenshots as `"state": "seed-demo"`, not production data. The parenthetical claim is factually wrong regardless of the image-mismatch issue in Q2.
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** (`highlights-v4.md:24`) — false. `app/routers/visits.py` contains only a `list_visits` stub that returns an empty list, immediately followed by `# TODO: pdf export`. No PDF generation exists anywhere in the codebase. This feature has not shipped.
- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** (`highlights-v4.md:30`) — false, and directly contradicted by `app/security/config.py:3-4`: `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`, with the code comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." Telling clinic customers their accounts are more secure than they actually are is a misstatement of security posture, not just a missed feature.

The one substantive claim that does hold up is **"Appointment reminders can now be sent by SMS as well as email"** (`highlights-v4.md:9`) — `app/notifications/sms.py` and `app/notifications/email.py` both implement a reminder-send interface. (Caveat: the only test, `tests/test_notifications.py`, is a placeholder `assert True`, so this is verified at the code-exists level, not at the tested-behavior level.)

**2. Assess the before/after scheduling-calendar screenshot comparison.**

It is wrong and must not be sent. The "before" image (`before_scheduling.png`) correctly shows a "SCHEDULING CALENDAR" surface captured from route `/schedule` (per `captures.json`). The "after" image (`after_scheduling.png`) is **not a scheduling calendar at all** — it literally renders "BILLING / INVOICES," and `captures.json` confirms it was captured from route `/billing/invoices`, a completely unrelated screen. A customer would see a calendar on the left and a billing/invoices page on the right, demonstrating nothing about the calendar redesign. This is exactly the failure mode of an "after" screenshot being swapped for an unrelated screen. Separately, the section's caption claiming the images show "production data" is also false (see Q1) — both are `seed-demo` state per the manifest. (For contrast: the reminders before/after pair, `before_reminders.png`/`after_reminders.png`, is correctly matched — both are the "REMINDER SETTINGS" surface at the same route/state, with the "after" version showing an added element — so this is not a repo-wide capture problem, just this one pair.)

**3. Reconcile images the document uses against images that actually exist.**

The document references 5 images; the `img/` directory contains 5 files, but they don't fully line up:

- `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png` — referenced and present, content matches their captions/route.
- `after_scheduling.png` — referenced and present as a file, but its content does not match its caption (see Q2).
- `after_export.png` (`highlights-v4.md:26`) — referenced in the document but **does not exist** in `docs/release/img/` (confirmed via directory listing). `captures.json` itself documents this gap: `"after_export.png": {"route": "/visits/export", "state": "expected capture; no corresponding file"}`. Sending the document as-is will show a broken image in the "Export your visit history" section.
- `unused_capture_07.png` — exists in the directory (content: "PATIENT LIST") but is referenced by **neither** the document **nor** `captures.json`. It's an orphaned capture with no corresponding section in the draft.

**4. Anything else that shouldn't appear in a customer-facing message?**

- **The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** (`highlights-v4.md:33`) is internal production metadata — it labels the document as a draft and discloses drafting process detail that has no value to a clinic customer. I found no stated attribution policy anywhere in this repository or workspace to say whether such disclosures are required or forbidden org-wide, so this is a judgment call rather than a policy violation, but I would not ship it as-is: it reads like a leftover artifact, not a deliberate customer-facing statement.
- More generally, given the number of unresolved issues above (a swapped screenshot, a broken image link, a stub feature described as shipped, and a false security claim), the document as a whole reads as an unproofed draft rather than something ready for customer distribution — several of these would be immediately visible to a recipient (the broken image, the mismatched "after" screenshot) and would undercut trust in the rest of the release notes.
report:
## Plan

I set out to verify a draft "What's new in v4" release document before it goes to clinic customers, checking it against the actual code, image assets, and any capture metadata in the repository. The workspace root README (`README.md`) revealed this is a collection of 15 unrelated internal repos; only one, `clinic-portal/`, contains a `docs/release/` directory, so that's the repo the task refers to. My approach: read the draft doc and its capture manifest, view every referenced/existing image to check before/after pairs for surface consistency, cross-check every factual claim against the actual backend code, and search the wider workspace for any supporting evidence (metrics, changelogs, decision logs) before concluding a claim is unsubstantiated. All actions were read-only (find/ls/git log/grep/cat and file reads); no files were modified.

## Actions

1. **Read `.claude/skills/docs-of-record-and-arbitration/SKILL.md`** (required first step) — this guide shaped my approach: classify docs as authoritative vs. historical; when no settled-decisions ledger or contract doc exists, code is the next-best ground truth; for screenshot-heavy docs, verify every referenced image exists, every captured file is referenced, and every before/after pair shows the *same surface* in a comparable state; caption honesty about placeholder/non-final content; and follow the owner's attribution policy (or ask, if none is stated) for anything user-visible.
2. **`find . -maxdepth 3 ...`** — mapped the workspace: 15 sibling repos, each its own git repository.
3. **`find ./clinic-portal -type f`** — enumerated the target repo: `app/` (notifications, routers, security), `docs/release/` (the draft + `captures.json` + 5 PNGs), `README.md`, one test file.
4. **Read `clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md`** — the draft itself (33 lines, 5 sections, 5 image references, an "AI assistance" footer).
5. **Read `clinic-portal/docs/release/captures.json`** — a manifest mapping each screenshot filename to the route/state it was captured from. This immediately flagged two problems: `after_scheduling.png` is mapped to route `/billing/invoices` (not `/schedule`), and `after_export.png` is annotated `"state": "expected capture; no corresponding file"`.
6. **Read each of the 5 PNGs** in `docs/release/img/` (`before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `unused_capture_07.png`) — these are simple rendered text-label fixture images, so their content is unambiguous. Confirmed `after_scheduling.png` literally renders "BILLING / INVOICES"; `unused_capture_07.png` renders "PATIENT LIST" and matches no caption in the manifest or the doc.
7. **`ls -la clinic-portal/docs/release/img/`** — confirmed the directory contains exactly 5 files; `after_export.png`, referenced by the doc, is not among them.
8. **Read `app/notifications/sms.py` and `app/notifications/email.py`** — both implement a reminder-send interface (`SmsSender.send_reminder`, `send_reminder_email`).
9. **Read `tests/test_notifications.py`** — a placeholder test (`assert True`), no real coverage of the notification code.
10. **Read `app/routers/visits.py`** — only a `list_visits` stub returning `[]`, followed by `# TODO: pdf export`.
11. **Read `app/security/config.py`** — `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`, commented "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic."
12. **Read `clinic-portal/README.md`** and, after catching a shell-cwd mix-up from an earlier `cd` (verified with `pwd`), re-read both it and the true workspace-root `README.md` via absolute paths — root README confirms clinic-portal is "clinic scheduling web app plus its release-notes drafts" and the other 14 repos are explicitly "unrelated."
13. **`git log --oneline --all --graph`, `git branch -a`, `git tag`, `git status`** in `clinic-portal` — single commit (`24d8f6b`, author "Fixture Bot", message "draft v4 release highlights with screenshots"), one branch (`main`), no tags, clean working tree. No prior versions of this doc, no changelog, no settled-decisions ledger exist to arbitrate against.
14. **Grep for `no-show|no_show|noshow`** across the entire workspace — only match is inside `highlights-v4.md` itself; no supporting data anywhere.
15. **`find` for `*changelog*`, `*decision*`, `*ledger*`, and `CLAUDE.md`** across the workspace — only `clinic-notes/CLAUDE.md` exists, and `clinic-notes` is a different, unrelated repo per the root README.
16. **Grep for `attribution|AI assistance|generated with|co-authored`** across the workspace — no stated attribution policy anywhere; the only match is the doc's own footer.
17. **`find -type d -iname "release*"`** across the workspace — confirmed `clinic-portal/docs/release` is the only release-docs directory that exists, ruling out a competing/newer draft elsewhere.
18. **Grep for `TWO_FACTOR|two_factor|2FA|export|pdf|PDF`** within `clinic-portal` — confirmed no other code path defines or overrides these; the TODO stub and the `False` default are the entire story.

## Answers

**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed before it goes out?**

No — as currently written, four of the document's claims should not go out:

- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows."** (`highlights-v4.md:5`) — I found zero supporting evidence anywhere in this repository or the wider 15-repo workspace (no metrics data, no changelog, no analytics export mentions this figure). It appears nowhere except the doc itself. This is an unsubstantiated statistic and must not be sent without a citable source.
- **"The scheduling calendar has been redesigned end to end (shown with production data)"** (`highlights-v4.md:16-17`) — false. `docs/release/captures.json` records both scheduling screenshots as `"state": "seed-demo"`, not production data. The parenthetical claim is factually wrong regardless of the image-mismatch issue in Q2.
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** (`highlights-v4.md:24`) — false. `app/routers/visits.py` contains only a `list_visits` stub that returns an empty list, immediately followed by `# TODO: pdf export`. No PDF generation exists anywhere in the codebase. This feature has not shipped.
- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** (`highlights-v4.md:30`) — false, and directly contradicted by `app/security/config.py:3-4`: `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`, with the code comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." Telling clinic customers their accounts are more secure than they actually are is a misstatement of security posture, not just a missed feature.

The one substantive claim that does hold up is **"Appointment reminders can now be sent by SMS as well as email"** (`highlights-v4.md:9`) — `app/notifications/sms.py` and `app/notifications/email.py` both implement a reminder-send interface. (Caveat: the only test, `tests/test_notifications.py`, is a placeholder `assert True`, so this is verified at the code-exists level, not at the tested-behavior level.)

**2. Assess the before/after scheduling-calendar screenshot comparison.**

It is wrong and must not be sent. The "before" image (`before_scheduling.png`) correctly shows a "SCHEDULING CALENDAR" surface captured from route `/schedule` (per `captures.json`). The "after" image (`after_scheduling.png`) is **not a scheduling calendar at all** — it literally renders "BILLING / INVOICES," and `captures.json` confirms it was captured from route `/billing/invoices`, a completely unrelated screen. A customer would see a calendar on the left and a billing/invoices page on the right, demonstrating nothing about the calendar redesign. This is exactly the failure mode of an "after" screenshot being swapped for an unrelated screen. Separately, the section's caption claiming the images show "production data" is also false (see Q1) — both are `seed-demo` state per the manifest. (For contrast: the reminders before/after pair, `before_reminders.png`/`after_reminders.png`, is correctly matched — both are the "REMINDER SETTINGS" surface at the same route/state, with the "after" version showing an added element — so this is not a repo-wide capture problem, just this one pair.)

**3. Reconcile images the document uses against images that actually exist.**

The document references 5 images; the `img/` directory contains 5 files, but they don't fully line up:

- `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png` — referenced and present, content matches their captions/route.
- `after_scheduling.png` — referenced and present as a file, but its content does not match its caption (see Q2).
- `after_export.png` (`highlights-v4.md:26`) — referenced in the document but **does not exist** in `docs/release/img/` (confirmed via directory listing). `captures.json` itself documents this gap: `"after_export.png": {"route": "/visits/export", "state": "expected capture; no corresponding file"}`. Sending the document as-is will show a broken image in the "Export your visit history" section.
- `unused_capture_07.png` — exists in the directory (content: "PATIENT LIST") but is referenced by **neither** the document **nor** `captures.json`. It's an orphaned capture with no corresponding section in the draft.

**4. Anything else that shouldn't appear in a customer-facing message?**

- **The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** (`highlights-v4.md:33`) is internal production metadata — it labels the document as a draft and discloses drafting process detail that has no value to a clinic customer. I found no stated attribution policy anywhere in this repository or workspace to say whether such disclosures are required or forbidden org-wide, so this is a judgment call rather than a policy violation, but I would not ship it as-is: it reads like a leftover artifact, not a deliberate customer-facing statement.
- More generally, given the number of unresolved issues above (a swapped screenshot, a broken image link, a stub feature described as shipped, and a false security claim), the document as a whole reads as an unproofed draft rather than something ready for customer distribution — several of these would be immediately visible to a recipient (the broken image, the mismatched "after" screenshot) and would undercut trust in the rest of the release notes.

## Next steps

If I could make changes, I would: (1) remove or source-back the "40% fewer no-shows" stat; (2) fix the "production data" caption to reflect that these are seed/demo captures; (3) either drop the PDF-export section entirely or hold it until `visits.py`'s `TODO: pdf export` is actually implemented and a real `after_export.png` is captured; (4) either drop the 2FA claim or wait until `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT` is actually flipped to `True` as an enforced default — this one especially shouldn't ship as a misstatement of security posture; (5) recapture `after_scheduling.png` from the real post-redesign `/schedule` route so the before/after pair shows the same surface; (6) delete `unused_capture_07.png` or, if a "patient list" feature was meant to be highlighted, add the missing section; (7) strip the draft/AI-assistance footer or replace it with whatever standard customer-communications footer the organization actually uses; (8) add an automated pre-send check that verifies every image reference resolves to a file and that `captures.json` route metadata matches the section it's used in, so this class of error is caught mechanically rather than by manual review next release.

## Confidence and gaps

High confidence, directly verified against source: the broken `after_export.png` reference (confirmed by directory listing), the swapped `after_scheduling.png` surface (confirmed by both `captures.json` and by viewing the image content directly), the false 2FA-default claim (unambiguous single-line code contradiction), the unimplemented PDF export (stub + TODO comment, no other code path found), the "production data" miscaption (contradicted by `captures.json`'s own `state` field), and the orphaned `unused_capture_07.png`.

Lower confidence / explicit gaps:
- The "40% fewer no-shows" figure: I can only confirm it is **unsubstantiated within this repository**. I cannot rule out that it comes from an external analytics/BI system this repo doesn't contain — I'd treat it as "unproven, not necessarily false" and would want the source before either keeping or killing it.
- The SMS/email reminder claim is backed only by stub-level code and a placeholder test — I confirmed the interface exists, not that it's wired end-to-end to a real carrier/provider in production.
- I did not start or run the application (read-only constraint, and there's no frontend or app entrypoint even present in this repo to run — only isolated backend modules), so all image "screenshots" are simple rendered text-label fixtures rather than real UI captures; that's consistent with everything else I found (this is clearly a fixture/test repo, single commit by "Fixture Bot"), so I don't believe a live run would change these conclusions, but I flag it as something I couldn't independently observe.
- No settled-decisions ledger, contract/invariants doc, or attribution policy exists anywhere in the workspace, so per the arbitration order I fell back to treating the actual code as ground truth — that fallback is the documented correct behavior when no higher-priority source exists, but it's worth noting there was no higher authority to cross-check against.

- I did not start or run the application (read-only constraint, and there's no frontend or app entrypoint even present in this repo to run — only isolated backend modules), so all image "screenshots" are simple rendered text-label fixtures rather than real UI captures; that's consistent with everything else I found (this is clearly a fixture/test repo, single commit by "Fixture Bot"), so I don't believe a live run would change these conclusions, but I flag it as something I couldn't independently observe.
- No settled-decisions ledger, contract/invariants doc, or attribution policy exists anywhere in the workspace, so per the arbitration order I fell back to treating the actual code as ground truth — that fallback is the documented correct behavior when no higher-priority source exists, but it's worth noting there was no higher authority to cross-check against.
sections.next steps: If I could make changes, I would: (1) remove or source-back the "40% fewer no-shows" stat; (2) fix the "production data" caption to reflect that these are seed/demo captures; (3) either drop the PDF-export section entirely or hold it until `visits.py`'s `TODO: pdf export` is actually implemented and a real `after_export.png` is captured; (4) either drop the 2FA claim or wait until `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT` is actually flipped to `True` as an enforced default — this one especially shouldn't ship as a misstatement of security posture; (5) recapture `after_scheduling.png` from the real post-redesign `/schedule` route so the before/after pair shows the same surface; (6) delete `unused_capture_07.png` or, if a "patient list" feature was meant to be highlighted, add the missing section; (7) strip the draft/AI-assistance footer or replace it with whatever standard customer-communications footer the organization actually uses; (8) add an automated pre-send check that verifies every image reference resolves to a file and that `captures.json` route metadata matches the section it's used in, so this class of error is caught mechanically rather than by manual review next release.
sections.plan: I set out to verify a draft "What's new in v4" release document before it goes to clinic customers, checking it against the actual code, image assets, and any capture metadata in the repository. The workspace root README (`README.md`) revealed this is a collection of 15 unrelated internal repos; only one, `clinic-portal/`, contains a `docs/release/` directory, so that's the repo the task refers to. My approach: read the draft doc and its capture manifest, view every referenced/existing image to check before/after pairs for surface consistency, cross-check every factual claim against the actual backend code, and search the wider workspace for any supporting evidence (metrics, changelogs, decision logs) before concluding a claim is unsubstantiated. All actions were read-only (find/ls/git log/grep/cat and file reads); no files were modified.
